Title: The MdVQ37-MdWRKY100 complex regulates salicylic acid content and MdRPM1 expression to modulate resistance to Glomerella leaf spot in apples

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates Glomerella leaf spot resistance and is associated with increased SA accumulation/content
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression; RNAi silencing; pathogen inoculation; lesion area measurement; SA quantification; RT-qPCR
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdRPM1 was identified as a direct transcriptional target of MdWRKY100. 2. Transient overexpression of MdRPM1 reduced lesion area after Colletotrichum fructicola infection. 3. Silencing MdRPM1 increased lesion area and altered endogenous free and total SA contents, supporting a positive role in GLS resistance.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by Colletotrichum fructicola infection in leaves
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Colletotrichum fructicola inoculation (Glomerella leaf spot infection)
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense signaling metabolite involved in pathogen resistance
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: MdRPM1 is not a biosynthetic enzyme for SA; it is a resistance gene whose manipulation altered SA levels during infection.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate resistance gene for improving GLS resistance, potentially combined with MdWRKY100 activation while monitoring defense-growth tradeoffs.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: DAP-seq for upstream regulator MdWRKY100 target identification; data available in supplementary material

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression decreases SA content and increases susceptibility to Glomerella leaf spot
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable overexpression; pathogen inoculation; lesion area measurement; PAL activity assay; SA quantification; Y2H; BiFC; split-LUC; pull-down; dual-luciferase assay
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdVQ37 overexpression increased susceptibility to Colletotrichum fructicola. 2. MdVQ37 overexpression reduced PAL activity and free/total SA contents. 3. MdVQ37 physically interacts with MdWRKY100 and represses MdWRKY100 transcriptional activity toward MdPAL1, MdRPM1, and MdWRKY17 promoters.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by Colletotrichum fructicola infection at 48 and 72 h in leaves
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Colletotrichum fructicola inoculation (Glomerella leaf spot infection)
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense signaling metabolite involved in pathogen resistance
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: MdVQ37 is a negative modulator of SA accumulation indirectly through repression of MdWRKY100 activity rather than a direct SA metabolic enzyme.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Negative target for knockout/editing to elevate SA-associated GLS resistance.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Previous studies cited in the paper showed MdVQ37 overexpression reduced drought, salt, and heat tolerance.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Salicylic acid biosynthesis via the phenylalanine ammonia-lyase pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Deaminates L-phenylalanine to trans-cinnamic acid, supplying the PAL-dependent salicylic acid biosynthetic route
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key enzyme gene of SA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S-driven pCambia2300 overexpression construct; pK7GWIWG2D RNAi construct
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increases PAL activity, SA content, and GLS resistance; silencing decreases SA content and resistance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression; RNAi silencing; PAL activity assay; SA quantification; lesion assay after pathogen inoculation; RT-qPCR; Y1H; EMSA
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdPAL1 is directly activated by MdWRKY100 through promoter binding. 2. Overexpression of MdPAL1 increased PAL activity and free/total SA contents, reducing lesion area after C. fructicola infection. 3. Silencing MdPAL1 lowered SA accumulation and increased GLS susceptibility.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated by Colletotrichum fructicola infection in leaves
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Colletotrichum fructicola inoculation (Glomerella leaf spot infection)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense signaling metabolite involved in pathogen resistance
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper explicitly describes MdPAL1 as a key enzyme gene of SA synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising engineering target to raise SA-mediated disease resistance; may be stacked with regulators such as MdWRKY100.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: DAP-seq for upstream regulator MdWRKY100 target identification; data available in supplementary material

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: SA biosynthesis/degradation balance and defense-gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses MdWRKY17; activates MdPAL1 and MdRPM1
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive regulator of SA accumulation and GLS resistance by simultaneously promoting SA biosynthesis, limiting SA degradation via MdWRKY17 repression, and activating MdRPM1
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Direct promoter binding to W-box elements leading to transcriptional activation or repression
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increases PAL activity, free SA, total SA, and resistance to Colletotrichum fructicola; RNAi decreases them
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable overexpression; RNAi silencing; pathogen assay; SA quantification; PAL activity assay; DAP-seq; Y1H; EMSA; RT-qPCR; dual-luciferase assay
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdWRKY100 is induced by C. fructicola infection and positively regulates apple resistance to GLS. 2. MdWRKY100 directly binds W-box-containing promoters of MdWRKY17, MdPAL1, and MdRPM1. 3. MdWRKY100 represses MdWRKY17 while activating MdPAL1 and MdRPM1, resulting in elevated SA accumulation and enhanced disease resistance.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense signaling metabolite involved in pathogen resistance
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper frames MdWRKY100 as the central positive regulator controlling both SA metabolic genes and a resistance gene during GLS infection.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for enhancing GLS resistance via overexpression or activation/editing, potentially combined with removal of MdVQ37 repression.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: DAP-seq using mature Gala leaf genomic DNA; supplementary material data availability

Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: MdDMR6 (reported in this paper's discussion from previous work)
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Positive regulatory factor of SA degradation; expression is repressed by MdWRKY100
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Acts as a susceptibility-associated regulator promoting SA degradation, thereby lowering SA-linked defense
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Its repression by MdWRKY100 is associated with increased SA accumulation and GLS resistance
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter binding by MdWRKY100 shown by DAP-seq, Y1H, EMSA; expression analysis in MdWRKY100 and MdVQ37 transgenic lines
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdWRKY17 is a direct target of MdWRKY100. 2. MdWRKY100 represses MdWRKY17 expression, while MdVQ37 relieves this repression by inhibiting MdWRKY100 activity. 3. The paper identifies MdWRKY17 as a positive regulatory factor gene of SA degradation relevant to GLS susceptibility.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: MdWRKY100 activity; indirectly MdVQ37 via repression of MdWRKY100; pathogen infection context
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Colletotrichum fructicola inoculation (context of GLS infection)
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense signaling metabolite involved in pathogen resistance
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: MdWRKY17 was not functionally re-manipulated in this paper but is included as a core regulatory target directly tested in the Results and central to the SA mechanism.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential negative target for editing/downregulation to preserve SA and improve GLS resistance.
